original_hypothesis.title: Unnecessary Max Approval to LiquidationPair Exposes Underlying Assets
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The `setLiquidationPair` function grants the configured LiquidationPair contract unlimited approval to spend the vault's underlying assets. However, the liquidation flow only involves minting vault shares and transferring prize tokens; the LP never needs to pull underlying assets from the vault.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: If the LiquidationPair contract is compromised, or if a malicious owner sets a rogue LP, the attacker can use the max approval to call `transferFrom` and drain all underlying assets currently held by the vault (e.g., assets in transit or idle).
preconditions: LiquidationPair is compromised or owner is malicious/compromised

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Approval of underlying asset instead of prize token or no approval
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker uses the approval to drain the vault
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker steals 100% of the vault's underlying asset balance

economic_questions: Does the actual LiquidationPair implementation ever need this approval?
validation_requirements: Review LiquidationPair code to confirm it never pulls underlying assets

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: All underlying assets in the vault

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: When vault holds significant idle assets

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All underlying assets in the vault

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
